B2B marketplace and ZATCA-compliant docu: B2B marketplace and ZATCA-compliant document platform — sync products, orders, merchants and invoices with Saudi Arabia's leading wholesale network.
Pick the Qawafel environment your API ke: Pick the Qawafel environment your API key belongs to.
\n**How to get your Qawafel API Key:**\n:
**How to get your Qawafel API Key:**

1. Sign in to your [Qawafel dashboard](https://qawafel.sa).
2. Open **Settings → Developers → API Keys**.
3. Click **Create API Key**, give it a name (e.g. "Activepieces") and copy the key.
4. Paste the key below. Keep it secret — anyone with the key can read and write your Qawafel data.

**Environment:**

- **Production** — live Qawafel tenant at `core.qawafel.sa`.
- **Development** — sandbox tenant at `core.development.qawafel.dev` for testing without touching live data.

API keys are scoped to a single environment. Pick the same one the key was created in.
Add a new product to your Qawafel catalo: Add a new product to your Qawafel catalog. The workhorse for catalog sync from your ERP or storefront into Qawafel.
Edit a product's price, description, or: Edit a product's price, description, or active state. Only the fields you fill in are updated — leave the rest blank to keep them unchanged.
Fetch a single product by its Qawafel ID: Fetch a single product by its Qawafel ID. Returns the full product including price, descriptions, and active status.
Get products from your catalog, with opt: Get products from your catalog, with optional filters by type, supplier, SKU, and active status. Returns up to 500 products (5 pages of 100).
Sync a completed (fulfilled) order into: Sync a completed (fulfilled) order into Qawafel. Use this to import historical or externally-completed orders from another storefront, ERP, or B2B portal — the order is created directly in **Fulfilled** state and skips the normal fulfilment workflow. Do **not** use this for new orders that still need to be confirmed, picked, and delivered.
Move an order forward in the Qawafel ful: Move an order forward in the Qawafel fulfilment workflow. Qawafel enforces a strict state machine — for example, an order must be **Out for Delivery** before it can be marked **Delivered**. To cancel an order, use the **Cancel Order** action instead.
Cancel an open order. Once cancelled, th: Cancel an open order. Once cancelled, the order cannot be reopened — the only path forward is to create a new order.
Fetch a single order by its Qawafel ID. Returns the full order including line items, totals, delivery details, and current state.
Get orders, optionally filtered by statu: Get orders, optionally filtered by status, customer, or creation date. Returns up to 500 orders (5 pages of 100).
Add a new customer or supplier to your Q: Add a new customer or supplier to your Qawafel tenant. Use this when onboarding a new B2B partner from your CRM, signup form, or another system.
Edit a merchant's trade name or active s: Edit a merchant's trade name or active state. Only the fields you fill in are updated. (Legal name, CR, VAT and other identity fields cannot be changed via the API.)
Create an invoice in Qawafel for a custo: Create an invoice in Qawafel for a customer. The invoice starts in `draft` state — use the **Generate Invoice** action (or call it from your dashboard) to finalize and issue a ZATCA-compliant copy.
Fetch a single invoice by its Qawafel ID: Fetch a single invoice by its Qawafel ID. Returns the full invoice including line items, totals, ZATCA PDF URL, and current state.
Get invoices, optionally filtered by sta: Get invoices, optionally filtered by status, customer, or creation date. Returns up to 500 invoices (5 pages of 100).
Make a custom API call to a specific end: Make a custom API call to a specific endpoint
Reason (only used when marking Not Deliv: Reason (only used when marking Not Delivered)
Pick **Sale** if this is a product you s: Pick **Sale** if this is a product you sell. Pick **Purchase** if it is a product you buy from a supplier (you must also pick the supplier below).
Stock Keeping Unit — your unique product: Stock Keeping Unit — your unique product code. Must be unique within your Qawafel tenant.
Product name in English. Shown on invoic: Product name in English. Shown on invoices and quotations.
Product name in Arabic. Required by Qawa: Product name in Arabic. Required by Qawafel for ZATCA-compliant invoices.
Default price per unit in Saudi Riyals a: Default price per unit in Saudi Riyals as a decimal string with two places, e.g. `99.00` or `1234.56`.
Required when **Product Type** is `Purch: Required when **Product Type** is `Purchase`. Pick the supplier merchant this product is bought from. Leave blank for `Sale` products.
Tick this if 15% VAT applies to this pro: Tick this if 15% VAT applies to this product. Untick for VAT-exempt items.
Optional. Longer description shown on do: Optional. Longer description shown on documents.
Optional. UPC, EAN or GTIN barcode.
Active products show up in lookups and c: Active products show up in lookups and can be sold. Untick to soft-disable.
Optional. Store an ID from another syste: Optional. Store an ID from another system here (e.g. your ERP record ID) to link records across systems. Must be unique within Qawafel.
Advanced. A UUID v4 — if the flow retrie: Advanced. A UUID v4 — if the flow retries this step, sending the same key prevents creating a duplicate. Most users can leave this blank.
Pick the product you want to change. Onl: Pick the product you want to change. Only the 100 most recent products are listed — use "List Products" if you need to find an older one.
Optional. New price per unit as a decima: Optional. New price per unit as a decimal string, e.g. `129.00`. Leave blank to keep the current price.
Optional. New English description. Leave: Optional. New English description. Leave blank to keep the current one.
Optional. New Arabic description. Leave: Optional. New Arabic description. Leave blank to keep the current one.
Optional. Tick to keep the product activ: Optional. Tick to keep the product active, untick to soft-disable. Leave blank to keep the current value.
The Qawafel product ID (starts with `pro: The Qawafel product ID (starts with `prod_`). You can find this on a product page in Qawafel, in the output of "List Products", or from a webhook trigger.
Optional. `Sale` returns products you se: Optional. `Sale` returns products you sell. `Purchase` returns products you buy from suppliers. Leave blank to return both.
Optional. Return only the product with t: Optional. Return only the product with this exact SKU (case-sensitive).
Optional. Only applies when filtering Pu: Optional. Only applies when filtering Purchase products. Pass a merchant ID (starts with `mer_`).
Tick to return only active products, unt: Tick to return only active products, untick for inactive only. Leave blank to return both.
Optional. Return only products created a: Optional. Return only products created after this date and time.
The customer placing the order. Pick fro: The customer placing the order. Pick from your existing customer merchants — use "Create Merchant" first if they are new.
One row per product on this order. Each: One row per product on this order. Each row needs a product, quantity, and unit price.
Postal code (5 digits in Saudi Arabia).
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. `S: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. `SA` for Saudi Arabia, `AE` for the UAE.
Optional. The Saudi National Address sho: Optional. The Saudi National Address short code (4 uppercase letters + 4 digits, e.g. `RHRA1234`).
Pick **Vendor** if you (the seller) deli: Pick **Vendor** if you (the seller) deliver yourself, or **Courier** to use a delivery service.
Optional. Delivery fees as a decimal str: Optional. Delivery fees as a decimal string, e.g. `15.00`. Required if you set a delivery method.
Required if you set a delivery method. P: Required if you set a delivery method. Pick **Customer** to bill the buyer, or **Vendor** to absorb the fee.
Optional. Internal notes attached to the: Optional. Internal notes attached to the order.
The Qawafel order ID (starts with `ord_`: The Qawafel order ID (starts with `ord_`). Get it from a webhook trigger, "List Orders", or your dashboard. Once you fill this in, the dropdown below only shows transitions valid for this order's current state.
Pick the next status. Only states reacha: Pick the next status. Only states reachable from the order's current state appear here. Workflow: Pending → Confirmed → Ready for Pickup → Out for Delivery → Delivered → Fulfilled.
Required when **New Status** is \"Mark N: Required when **New Status** is "Mark Not Delivered" — explains why delivery failed (max 500 characters). Ignored for all other transitions.
The Qawafel order ID (starts with `ord_`: The Qawafel order ID (starts with `ord_`) you want to cancel.
Optional but recommended. A short note e: Optional but recommended. A short note explaining why the order is being cancelled (max 500 characters).
The Qawafel order ID (starts with `ord_`: The Qawafel order ID (starts with `ord_`). You can get this from a trigger, the "List Orders" action, or your dashboard.
Optional. Return only orders in this sta: Optional. Return only orders in this state. Leave blank for all statuses.
Optional. Return only orders from this c: Optional. Return only orders from this customer. Leave blank for all customers.
Optional. Return only orders created aft: Optional. Return only orders created after this date and time.
Pick **Customer** if this party buys fro: Pick **Customer** if this party buys from you, or **Supplier** if you buy from them.
The official registered company name as: The official registered company name as it appears on the commercial registration.
The trade or store name in English. Show: The trade or store name in English. Shown on documents and dashboards.
The trade or store name in Arabic. Requi: The trade or store name in Arabic. Required by Qawafel for ZATCA-compliant invoices.
Primary contact email for this merchant.
Primary phone number, including country: Primary phone number, including country code (e.g. `+966500000000`).
Optional. 15-digit VAT registration numb: Optional. 15-digit VAT registration number, starts with `3`. Required if the merchant is VAT-registered.
Optional. Saudi Unified National Number: Optional. Saudi Unified National Number (الرقم الوطني الموحد), 10 digits starting with `7`.
Tick if this merchant is registered for: Tick if this merchant is registered for VAT. Affects how invoices to/from them are calculated.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. `S: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code, e.g. `SA` for Saudi Arabia.
Optional. National Address short code (4: Optional. National Address short code (4 letters + 4 digits, e.g. `RHRA1234`).
Pick the merchant to update. Use \"Creat: Pick the merchant to update. Use "Create Merchant" first if they are not yet in Qawafel.
Optional. Leave blank to keep the curren: Optional. Leave blank to keep the current name.
Optional. Tick to keep the merchant acti: Optional. Tick to keep the merchant active, untick to disable. Leave blank to keep the current value.
The date the invoice is issued. Use toda: The date the invoice is issued. Use today for most cases. (YYY-MM-DD format)
One row per product on this invoice. Eac: One row per product on this invoice. Each row needs a product, quantity, unit price, and VAT %.
Optional. The Qawafel order ID (`ord_…`): Optional. The Qawafel order ID (`ord_…`) this invoice is for. Linking helps track fulfilment-to-billing.
Optional. Delivery / shipping fees as a: Optional. Delivery / shipping fees as a decimal string excluding VAT, e.g. `25.00`. Defaults to `0.00`.
Optional. When the payment terms begin. (YYY-MM-DD format)
Optional. The absolute final date paymen: Optional. The absolute final date payment is due (used for late-payment workflows). (YYY-MM-DD format)
Optional. Notes printed on the invoice o: Optional. Notes printed on the invoice or kept internal.
The Qawafel invoice ID (starts with `inv: The Qawafel invoice ID (starts with `inv_`). Find this in a webhook payload, the "List Invoices" action, or the dashboard.
Optional. Return only invoices in this s: Optional. Return only invoices in this state. Leave blank for all statuses.
Optional. Return only invoices for this: Optional. Return only invoices for this customer. Leave blank for all customers.
Optional. Return only invoices created a: Optional. Return only invoices created after this date and time.
Authorization headers are injected autom: Authorization headers are injected automatically from your connection.
Enable for files like PDFs, images, etc.
Purchase (something you buy from a suppl: Purchase (something you buy from a supplier)
HEAD: HEAD
Fires the moment a new sales order is cr: Fires the moment a new sales order is created in Qawafel. Use it to fan out to fulfillment, accounting, Slack alerts, or your CRM.
Fires when an order moves to a specific: Fires when an order moves to a specific status (confirmed, out for delivery, delivered, fulfilled, cancelled, etc.). Pick one status per trigger — add the trigger again for additional statuses.
Fires when an invoice is marked as paid: Fires when an invoice is marked as paid in Qawafel. Useful for posting payment receipts to Slack, releasing fulfilment, or syncing revenue to your books.
Fires when a ZATCA-compliant invoice is: Fires when a ZATCA-compliant invoice is finalized in Qawafel (the `invoice.generated` event). Use it to email PDFs to customers, archive invoices to Drive, or push to your accounting system.
Fires when a new product is created in Q: Fires when a new product is created in Qawafel. Use it to mirror your catalog into Shopify, Salla, Zid, or your data warehouse.
Fires when a product is updated (price c: Fires when a product is updated (price change, description edit, activated/deactivated). Use it to keep external storefronts in sync.
Fires when a new merchant (customer or s: Fires when a new merchant (customer or supplier) is created in Qawafel. Use it to onboard customers in your CRM or add suppliers to your procurement system.
Fires when a credit note is created agai: Fires when a credit note is created against an invoice (i.e. a customer return or refund is initiated). Use it to alert your finance team or trigger a return workflow.
The flow runs every time an order transi: The flow runs every time an order transitions into the status you pick here.
